title: Saved connections not respecting specified logon credentials
author: hux2000
body_snippet:
<!-- Bug -->
|||
|--:|---|
|Operating system | Windows 10 Enterprise, x64, build 10586.164 |
|mRemoteNG version| 1.72.5065.32737  |

For saved RDP connections (I haven't tested with other types), if you connect to the system using the Microsoft RDP client, with a different username than the one stored in the mRemoteNG connection, mRemoteNG will thereafter use that username and not the one in the stored connection.

To reproduce:

1) Create a new stored RDP connection in mRemoteNG, spec...

iterations.description: Issue is stale with no reproducible steps provided, two triage refresh comments already posted with no actionable response from reporter, and the workaround suggests it may be an mstsc credential caching issue rather than an mRemoteNG bug.
Bulk verified: commit f34c13e7 exists (MEDIUM confidence)

comments.author: sparerd
comments.snippet: Whatever was going on in v1.72, I can't seem to replicate this in any v1.75 build. Are you able to replicate on newer versions?

comments.author: hux2000
comments.snippet:
I just uninstalled 1.72, installed 1.75 (stable, hotfix 1) and replicated the behavior per the above.

I erred slightly in the steps I listed above, though: steps 2 and 3 should both say "; disconnect from the system" at the end. (I thought that would be implied, but maybe not.)

Basically, for me mRemoteNG doesn't really respect the configured user at all. (For RDP connections, at least. I haven't tested other types.) It doesn't matter what I put in Connection -> Username, it will always co...

comments.author: hux2000
comments.snippet: FYI, I just installed version 1.75.7010.21198 (portable) and am still experiencing the issue I described above.

comments.author: CAT-hs
comments.snippet:
I had this problem over the weekend, and after updating to the latest version and still having the problem I found this thread.
Since there was no fix or workaround posted, figured I'd share my workaround: I opened mstsc.exe, make sure show options is expanded, selected each computer in the Computer drop down list and made sure whatever option is under the user name box was set to counter use of saved credentials and clicked Save before moving on to the next computer in the drop down list or clo...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

comments.snippet:

**Commit:** `f34c13e7` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** Saved connections not respecting specified logon credentials

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

notes:
AI triage: Issue is stale with no reproducible steps provided, two triage refresh comments already posted with no actionable response from reporter, and the workaround suggests it may be an mstsc credential caching issue rather than an mRemoteNG bug.
Approach: If reproduced, investigate whether RDP connection credentials from ConnectionInfo are correctly passed to the MSTSC ActiveX control during session initialization.
